body:
Jeff,

The wake-up call hasn't been coming through the past few mornings. Three days in a row, best I can count. Figured you'd want to know rather than just let it go.

I'm up on my own — I've got Dale waiting on me at Koffee Kup Kafe around seven, so I don't exactly sleep in. Still. I set the service up for a reason, and it ought to work the way it's supposed to.

Let me know if you need anything from my end.

Gary Pittman
Aberdeen

Gary,

Thank you for writing in. I pulled up your account and found the issue — there was a system update last Thursday that knocked several schedules off the delivery queue. Yours was one of them. That's on us, not anything you did.

I've reset your wake-up call for 6:30 AM Central, Monday through Friday, same as your original setup. You should get it tomorrow morning. If it doesn't come through, just reply here or call the main line and we'll get right on it.

Appreciate your patience, Gary. And for what it's worth, Dale and Koffee Kup Kafe sound like a pretty reliable backup system.

Jeff Pittman
Customer Support

Jeff,

Hope you're doing all right. I'm writing about the morning medications — the Spiriva, lisinopril, vitamin D, and acetaminophen. I take them first thing, before coffee even. Been doing it that way a good while now.

Had a question come up. Ran a little low on the vitamin D this week. Wasn't sure if I needed to call the pharmacy myself or if that goes through your end. Didn't want to double up on calls and cause confusion.

Also wanted to make sure you had the right list. Sometimes things get updated and nobody tells anybody.

Gary Pittman

Gary,

Thank you for reaching out — glad you did before it became a problem. For the vitamin D, that one you can pick up over the counter at any pharmacy, no prescription needed. So you're free to grab a bottle on your own without going through us. Much simpler that way.

For the lisinopril and Spiriva, those do require a prescription refill. If you're getting close on either of those, let me know and I can send a heads-up to your prescribing provider to get the process started. Better to flag it early.

I do have your current medication list on file. It matches what you've described — Spiriva, lisinopril, vitamin D, and acetaminophen in the mornings. If anything changes, just send a note and I'll update it.

Don't hesitate to reach out anytime.

Jeff Pittman
Patient Support

Jeff,

That clears it up. I'll just grab the vitamin D at the store — probably stop by after coffee with Dale one of these mornings. We're at the Koffee Kup Kafe most days, so it's not out of the way.

I've got enough of the others for now. I'll keep an eye on them and let you know when things start getting short. Good to know the process ahead of time.

Appreciate you getting back to me quick. I've got a shift over at Bethlehem Lutheran this week, so it's good to have this sorted.

Gary

Jeff,

Had coffee with Dale this morning at Koffee Kup. He showed up twenty minutes late, which is not like him. Truck wouldn't start, he said. We ended up staying an extra half hour. Place was pretty full for a Monday — must have been some kind of meeting in the back room. Anyway, Dale's doing all right. He heard they might put a stoplight in out by the grain elevator on the highway. Said it's a waste of money. I didn't argue.

Dad

Dad,

A stoplight out there? That intersection's been a mess since I was a kid — I'm surprised it took this long, honestly. Did Dale say when they're starting on it? I'm also curious where he heard that from. Could just be co-op talk.

Also, how's he otherwise? Thought I heard from Karen a while back that his knee was giving him trouble.

Jeff

Jeff,

He heard it from somebody at the co-op. So who knows. Could be nothing. His knee is better — he was walking fine, didn't mention it at all. We split a piece of lemon cake they had today. It was good. Not as good as Karen's, but I wasn't about to say that sitting in public.

Tell Tyler I've still got that socket set he wanted to borrow. Whenever he's ready.

Dad

Dad,

Wise decision keeping that cake opinion to yourself. Karen would probably agree with you anyway but she'd make a whole thing out of it either way.

I'll tell Tyler about the socket set — he's been meaning to get up there. Maybe we'll make a trip before the summer's gone. Glad Dale's doing better. Sounds like a good morning.

Jeff

Jeff,

Checked the church calendar this morning, right after coffee with Dale. Something's not right. The fellowship hall has two groups booked on the 12th — looks like the ladies' circle and the council dinner got stacked on the same night. I noticed it when I was going through the custodial setup schedule.

Pastor Lindgren's been wrapped up with confirmation prep, so I didn't want to drop it on him. Thought you might know who handles the booking side of things, or who to nudge.

I can move tables around just fine, but I can't put two groups in one room.

Dad

Dad,

Glad you caught that. I reached out to Linda in the church office and she tracked it down — the council moved their dinner date last week and it got entered twice by mistake. She's already corrected it. Council dinner is moving to the 19th, so the ladies' circle has the hall to themselves on the 12th.

No chair crisis after all. I know that's probably a mild disappointment.

Next time you're at the Koffee Kup, tell Dale hello from me. And let me know if anything else looks off on that calendar — better you catch it on a Monday than someone else catch it on the night of.

Jeff

Hi Jeff,

Just following up on that Gmail business from last week. You walked me through something about connecting my account to the church calendar app and I thought it worked, but now it's asking me for an API key again. Karen looked at it Sunday after service and said it looks like the connection dropped.

I'm not sure what I did or didn't do. I followed the steps you wrote down. Maybe I missed something.

I'm over at the church most mornings this week. If you need me to try something, just say so and I'll give it a shot.

Gary

Hi Gary,

Thanks for following up — totally understandable that this is happening. What you're describing sounds like an OAuth token expiration. Basically, when you authorized the Gmail API connection, Google issued a short-lived token. If the app didn't store a refresh token properly, it loses access after a while and asks you to re-authorize.

Here's what I'd like you to try: open the calendar app, go to Settings, find the Gmail connection, and click "Disconnect." Then reconnect it fresh. This time, when Google shows you the permissions screen, make sure you click "Allow" on both screens — there are usually two. That should get it a refresh token it can hold onto.

If Karen is around and wants to help, that's fine too — it's pretty straightforward once you know where to look. Let me know how it goes and I'll stay close to my email.

Jeff

Jeff,

Well, it worked. Disconnected it, went through both permission screens like you said, and it's been holding steady since yesterday morning. Dale even asked me about it at the Koffee Kup this morning — he's got the same issue on his end with his church newsletter thing. I told him to call you.

I appreciate you sticking with it. I know it's not a big problem in the scheme of things, but the Monday calendar check is part of my routine and it's nice to have it working right.

Thanks again.
Gary Pittman
Bethlehem Lutheran

Jeff,

Followed up like you said. Went back into that Google Calendar thing on the church computer — the one in Pastor's office. Still not pulling in the new events from the API. Dale thinks I should just write everything on the paper calendar in the fellowship hall and call it done. He might be right.

Anyway, Karen said you'd know what to check. Something about a key expiring? I wrote down what the screen said. It says "403 forbidden" and then a bunch of other stuff I didn't copy down in time.

Let me know what you need from me.

Dad

Dad,

Thanks for writing that down — the 403 is actually helpful. That means the API key the church is using has either expired or lost permission to access the calendar. Happens more than you'd think.

Here's what I need you to do: on that same computer, open the browser and go to console.cloud.google.com. Log in with whatever Google account the church uses. Look for a project called something like "Bethlehem Calendar" and click on "Credentials." Tell me what you see under API Keys — specifically if any of them show a warning symbol.

Take your time. No rush on this. If it's easier, you could also just screenshot it and email it to me.

Talk soon,
Jeff

Jeff,

Okay. Found the credentials page. There's one key in there and it does have a little warning triangle next to it. Says "Key restricted" and then underneath that it shows the expiration date was last March. So I guess that's the problem.

Had to do all this after my shift this morning. Mopped the fellowship hall, fixed the hinge on the side door again — that thing's been fighting me since February. Then sat down at the computer. Roger said he could come help next week if we still haven't got it sorted, but I'd rather figure it out before that.

What do I do with the expired key?

Dad

Dad,

Yep, that's exactly it. The key expired and it's been bouncing requests ever since March. Easy fix from here.

On that same Credentials page, click the pencil icon next to the key to edit it. You'll see an "Expiration" section — either extend the date or set it to "Never expires" if the church doesn't want to deal with this again. Then hit Save. After that, give it about five minutes and the calendar should start syncing again. If it doesn't, try refreshing the page that pulls in the events.

Tell Roger he's off the hook. And tell Dale the paper calendar can stay as backup — that's just good sense.

Call me if it doesn't work.

Jeff

Hi Jeff,

Just following up on that Ring doorbell business we talked about. I got the app open again like you said, but it still won't pull up the camera feed on my phone. Just sits there spinning. Tried it at the church this morning before my shift and same thing.

Not sure if it's the wi-fi out here or what. Dale says his does the same thing sometimes but I don't know if that helps you any.

Let me know what you need from me. I got time after the Monday calendar check.

Dad

Hi Dad,

Thanks for checking back. That spinning screen usually means the app lost its connection to the device — pretty common fix. Go into the Ring app, tap the three lines in the top left, then Device Health on the doorbell. Tell me what it says under Network. If the signal strength is Poor or Very Poor, that's our answer right there.

Also, when you're at the church, the church wi-fi might be blocking the connection. Try it on cell data instead — just turn wi-fi off on your phone for a sec and see if the feed loads. Might save us a few steps.

If none of that shakes loose, I can walk you through a reconnect this evening. Just call after six.

Jeff

Jeff,

Still poking around with that weather API thing you helped me get started on. Checked it this morning after coffee with Dale over at Koffee Kup and it pulled the right temperature for Aberdeen, which I'll call a win. Problem is the wind speed is showing in meters per second. I don't know what that means and I don't plan to learn.

I do church work early on Mondays and I like to know if it's going to be blowing sideways before I drag equipment outside. That's the whole reason I wanted this thing set up. Miles per hour would suit me better.

Let me know if there's an easy fix or if I'm asking too much of it.

Gary

Gary,

Glad it's at least pulling the right city. The wind unit is a straightforward fix. In the API URL you're using, just add units=imperial as a parameter and it'll switch everything over to Fahrenheit and miles per hour automatically. Should take about thirty seconds to change.

If you can't find where the URL is, just let me know what device you're using and I'll walk you through it step by step. The free API key I set up for you should still be active — those don't expire unless the account goes dormant for a long stretch.

Reply back if you run into any trouble.

Jeff
Support Team

Jeff,

That did it. Added the imperial part and now it's reading miles per hour just fine. Showed Dale at Koffee Kup this morning and he said it was the smartest thing he'd seen in a while. That's a low bar coming from Dale, but I'll take it.

Karen called last night and asked what I'd been fussing with on my phone. Told her weather stuff for planning church work. She seemed satisfied with that. Roger probably would've wanted a full explanation but he wasn't on the call.

Appreciate the help. Simple fix, like you said.

Gary

Jeff,

Just following up on that wake-up call thing you set up last week. Tried it Monday morning before I walked over to the Koffee Kup to meet Dale. It rang fine. I was already up, but I picked up like you said.

Not sure if I'm supposed to do something to confirm it on my end or if it just logs automatically. Let me know what you need from me.

Dad

Dad,

Glad it rang. You do need to confirm — either pick up and stay on for a few seconds, or if it's the app version, tap the green button. That's what tells the system you're up and okay. If you just let it ring through, it flags as a missed check-in on my end.

I'll pull up Monday's log and see how it recorded. Should be straightforward to tell. I'll email you back once I look.

Jeff

Jeff,

Okay, I hit the green button this morning. Seemed to work. The whole thing took about fifteen seconds, which is fine by me.

Told Dale about it at the Koffee Kup. He said it sounded like something his daughter would set up too. I told him it beats getting a phone call at noon asking if I'm still alive. He agreed. Anyway — it's working.

One thing. Tuesday it came in about ten minutes late. Don't know if that's on my end or yours.

Dad

Dad,

Tuesday's log shows the delay was on the server side — nothing you did wrong. Happens occasionally, usually clears itself. If it starts coming in late regularly I can flag it, but one time is nothing to worry about.

The green button confirm is exactly right. That's all you need to do. I can see it logged clean this morning.

Tell Dale his daughter has good instincts. Talk soon.

Jeff

Hello,

I wanted to follow up on a question I sent in last week. I take four pills every morning — Spiriva, lisinopril, vitamin D, and acetaminophen. I take them all at once, usually while I'm having coffee over at Koffee Kup Kafe. That's just been the routine.

My pharmacist said something about spacing a couple of them out, but it was busy in there and I didn't get the full story. I'm not looking to overhaul anything. Just want to know if what I'm doing is fine or if I need to adjust.

Thanks for your time.

Gary Pittman
Aberdeen, SD

Hi Mr. Pittman,

Thanks for writing back. Your morning routine sounds reasonable. Taking those medications together with food or a drink — coffee counts — is generally fine and helps with absorption for a couple of them, especially the vitamin D.

The main thing with all four is consistency. Same time each day matters more than the exact minute. If your pharmacist had a specific concern about one of them interacting with another, it'd be worth a quick call to ask them to spell it out. Sometimes it's hard to catch everything at the counter.

You're doing the right thing by checking. Let us know if anything else comes up.

Best,
Jeff
Patient Support Line

Jeff,

Had coffee with Dale this morning at the Koffee Kup. He was already in the booth when I got there, which is new. Usually I beat him by five minutes. He said he's been sleeping poorly. Didn't say much more about it.

We talked about the church rummage sale and whether the parking lot needs resurfacing. Dale thinks it does. I told him that decision's above my pay grade. He laughed. We had a second cup.

Anyway. You asked last time how he was getting along. Now you know.

Dad

Dad,

Dale beating you there — that's something. That man has always run on farmer time. Is he okay otherwise? Sleeping poorly can mean a lot of things.

The rummage sale sounds like it's coming up fast. Are you doing setup again this year? I seem to remember you hauling folding tables around last summer. Tyler mentioned he might drive through Aberdeen sometime in the next few weeks. If the timing lines up, maybe he could lend a hand.

Let me know how the week shapes up. Good to hear from you.

Jeff

Jeff,

Dale seems fine. Just restless, he said. Been watching too much news. I told him to turn it off after seven. He said his wife tells him the exact same thing. So there you go.

Rummage sale is the 12th. I'll be in early to get tables set up. If Tyler comes through, send him over to the church — I'm there most Monday and Saturday mornings. Be good to see that kid.

That's about all I've got.

Dad

Jeff,

Checked the church calendar this morning like I do every Monday. Looks like there's a fellowship dinner on the calendar for Saturday the 11th, but nothing came through on the building-use sheet yet. I need that confirmed before I start hauling tables out of storage. Pastor Knutson usually sends it over, but I haven't seen it.

Also, Thursday's got a double-booking. Bible study and the quilting ladies are both in the fellowship hall at the same time. Somebody's going to show up with a hot dish and nowhere to put it.

Let me know.

Gary

Hi Gary,

Thanks for catching both of those — good thing you check every week. The fellowship dinner on the 11th is confirmed. I talked to Pastor Knutson and he admitted he forgot the building-use sheet. He'll have it to you by Wednesday at the latest.

For Thursday, I moved the Bible study to the smaller room off the narthex. Quilting ladies had it first, so they stay put. I've updated the calendar and notified both groups.

Appreciate you staying on top of this. Holler if anything else looks off.

Jeff

Jeff,

Good. I'll set up the fellowship hall Friday afternoon after my shift wraps up. Just need to know — eight-foot tables or six-foot? Makes a difference in how much I have to move around back there. If Pastor Knutson's note doesn't say, ask him.

I'll also make sure the heat's running in that narthex room before Thursday morning. It gets cold in there fast. Nobody's going to want to read Philippians with their coat on.

Gary

Gary,

Eight-foot tables — Pastor is expecting around forty people, give or take. He'll have a firm count to you by Thursday morning so you can adjust if needed.

And good call on the heat. I'll add that to the Thursday prep checklist so it doesn't fall through the cracks again.

Honestly, Gary, a lot of these things would go sideways if you weren't checking on Monday mornings. The church runs better because you pay attention. Thanks for keeping it all straight.

Jeff
